architecture.claim: Sub-charts read via sync `Get*OrNull` and slice the night-spanning entries to `DayWindowKey.Range` at paint time.
architecture.finding: `DayWindowKey` has no `.Range` property. The type exposes only `ChartStartUtcTicks`, `Count`, and the derived `ChartStartUtc` (E:\Projects\AI\TargetPlanner\TargetPlanner\Caches\DayWindowKey.cs:25-46). At paint time the Day sub-chart calls `ChartLayout.BuildDayWindow(night, zone)` which returns a tuple `(Key, ChartStart, ChartStop, StartUtc, EndUtc, Count)` and accesses `dayWindow.StartUtc`, `dayWindow.EndUtc`, `dayWindow.Count` directly (AltitudeSubChart_Day.cs:370-374) — no `.Range` call anywhere. Additionally, the per-night trajectory and moon entries are already sized to exactly `Window.Count` samples (`TargetTrajectoryEntry.Samples` and `MoonEphemerisEntry.Samples`), so no slicing of a larger 'night-spanning' array is performed at paint time.
